summary: Finished and linked the Articuno/Freezer item translation by switching src/melee/it/items/itfreezer.c from NonMatching to Matching, adding the local ItemStateTable, renaming remaining generic it_802Cxxxx symbols to itFreezer_* names, and correcting Freezer-specific attribute/item-var types. The PR also updated symbol records and callsites so the item logic table points at the newly named Freezer callbacks.
what_changed: Marked melee/it/items/itfreezer.c as Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
Renamed remaining generic Freezer functions in symbols.txt and headers from it_802CCF9C, it_802CCFFC, it_802CD000, it_802CD090, it_802CD12C, it_802CD290, and it_802CD2EC to itFreezer_* equivalents.
Added/defined ItemStateTable it_803F7C70 in itfreezer.c with three states wired to itFreezer_UnkMotion0/1/2 Anim, Phys, and Coll callbacks.
Updated the common item logic table in it_2725.c so the Freezer/Articuno entry calls itFreezer_802CCF9C and itFreezer_802CD000 rather than generic address names.
Renamed typo/case-inconsistent structs itfreezertAttributes and itfreezer_ItemVars to itFreezerAttributes and itFreezer_ItemVars.
Changed itFreezerAttributes field xC from f32 to s32, matching its use as a countdown/reset value for item->xDD4_itemVar.freezer.x60.
Updated Item.xDD4_itemVar.freezer union member type in src/melee/it/types.h to itFreezer_ItemVars.
Localized two sdata2 float symbols at 0x804DD3B0 and 0x804DD3B4 as @145 and @161 in symbols.txt instead of global it_804DD3B0 and it_804DD3B4.
Cleaned formatting and include ordering in itfreezer.c while preserving behavior: brace spacing, pointer style, and long attribute casts split across lines.
smart_moves: Renaming generic address-based symbols only after the file matched made the linked object easier to search and safer to call from shared item tables.
Adding the ItemStateTable directly in itfreezer.c tied the state-machine callbacks to the item implementation instead of relying on an external/nonmatching definition.
Correcting xC to s32 is evidence-based: it is assigned into freezer.x60 and used as a decrementing timer, so treating it as an integer avoids type friction and likely helped matching.
Kept public function address comments in itfreezer.h while changing names, preserving address traceability during decomp work.
Updated all dependent callsites and headers together, avoiding stale prototypes or item logic table references after symbol renaming.
decomp_lessons: When finishing an item file, check configure.py, symbols.txt, item logic tables, item-specific headers, common attribute structs, and Item union members as one linkage set.
If an article attribute field feeds a countdown or frame timer, verify whether the field should be s32 rather than f32 even when neighboring attributes are floats.
For Pokemon/common item files, matching may require recovering the item state table shape before the object can be linked as Matching.
Address-based names can be systematically upgraded to item-prefixed names once ownership is clear, but all symbol map entries and external references must move together.
Local float constants in sdata2 may need local @NN labels rather than global it_* object symbols to reflect compiler-emitted local constants.
naming_conventions: Freezer item functions use the item prefix and address suffix form: itFreezer_802CCF9C, itFreezer_802CD090, itFreezer_802CD2EC.
Motion callbacks use the pattern itFreezer_UnkMotionN_Anim, itFreezer_UnkMotionN_Phys, and itFreezer_UnkMotionN_Coll.
Item-specific structs use PascalCase item prefix with suffix: itFreezerAttributes and itFreezer_ItemVars.
The Item union member remains lowercase semantic name freezer while its type is item-prefixed: itFreezer_ItemVars freezer.
Avoid typo/lowercase type names like itfreezertAttributes and itfreezer_ItemVars once item ownership is identified.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed configure.py from NonMatching to Matching only for itfreezer.c, indicating the translation reached linkable matching status.
Used an inline/static helper split for repeated motion-transition setup: itFreezer_UnkMotion2_Anim_Inline and itFreezer_UnkMotion0_Phys_inline1/inline2 were retained to satisfy codegen structure.
Used explicit callback casts where required by existing APIs, e.g. it_8027A118(gobj, (void (*)(HSD_GObj*)) itFreezer_802CCFFC).
Preserved empty stub callback itFreezer_802CCFFC as a named no-op function, matching the 0x4 function in symbols.txt.
Kept item accessor and callback assignment patterns consistent: GET_ITEM(gobj), item->entered_hitlag = efLib_PauseAll, item->exited_hitlag = efLib_ResumeAll, and item->on_accessory = itFreezer_*.
follow_up_queries: Find other Pokemon/common item PRs that moved an item file from NonMatching to Matching and added an ItemStateTable.
Search for remaining lowercase or typo item attribute structs like itfreezertAttributes in common headers.
Search for item attribute fields used as timers but typed as f32.
Find other symbols.txt changes converting global it_* sdata2 objects into local @NN constants.
Compare itfreezer.c matching tactics with neighboring item files itthunder.c, itsonans.c, and ithassam.c.
